title: Directories created with overly permissive POSIX permissions (0o777)
description: Both createPath (line 627) and receiveUploadDirectory (ServerController.swift line 2009) create directories with POSIX permissions 0o777 (world-readable, writable, executable). The initFilesSystem method at line 611 also uses 0o777. On a shared server, this allows any local OS user to read, write, and delete files in the Wired file tree, bypassing all Wired-level access controls.
recommendation: Use restrictive permissions (e.g. 0o755 or 0o700) for created directories. Only the server process user should have write access. If the web server needs read access, use 0o750 with a shared group.
